name: GT-hyp
source: HYPOTHESIS BLOCK of lem-intertwine-spectral-idempotent (the lemma's own GIVENS / antecedents — not a refs claim, legitimately quote-less). GIVEN: (H1) A and B are unital Banach algebras with units denoted I (I_A in A, I_B in B); F is an element of A and Phi an element of B. As BANACH ALGEBRAS, their norms are SUBMULTIPLICATIVE: ||a a'|| <= ||a|| ||a'|| for all a,a' in A (the Banach-algebra norm axiom), and likewise in B; the units satisfy ||I_A||=||I_B||=1 and act as the identity operator under left/right multiplication (I_A . a = a = a . I_A). (H2) ||F^2-F|| < 1/4 (op-norm in A) and ||Phi^2-Phi|| < 1/4 (op-norm in B) — exactly the def-spectral-idempotent threshold, so theta(2F-1) in A and theta(2Phi-1) in B are both well-defined. (H3) j: B -> A is a BOUNDED LINEAR map between the underlying Banach spaces (NOT an element of either algebra), with finite operator norm ||j||_op = sup_{||y||<=1}||j(y)|| < infinity. (H4) j is UNITAL: j(I_B)=I_A. [NOTE: H4 is advertised by the consumer/contract but is NOT load-bearing in the proof; nodes 1.1-1.6 use only that I_A, I_B are multiplicative units (H1), not j(I_B)=I_A.] (H5) j INTERTWINES F and Phi: F j = j Phi (as bounded linear maps B->A, i.e. for all y in B, F(j(y))=j(Phi(y)); products F j, j Phi mean composition of the multiplication-operator / element-action with j). NOTE on products: throughout, an algebra element X (e.g. F, Phi, S_F, R_F) is identified with its action by left multiplication; 'X j' is the composite map B->A (apply j then multiply by X on the A-side), 'j Y' is B->A (apply Y on the B-side then j). DERIVED bound used in node 1.4 (element-times-j): for an element a in A and j bounded, the composite a j: B->A satisfies ||a j||_op <= ||a|| ||j||_op, since for ||y||<=1, ||(a j)(y)|| = ||a . j(y)|| <= ||a|| ||j(y)|| (H1 SUBMULTIPLICATIVITY in A) <= ||a|| ||j||_op, then sup over ||y||<=1; symmetrically ||j U||_op <= ||j||_op ||U|| for an element U in B (||j(U . y)|| <= ||j||_op ||U . y|| <= ||j||_op ||U|| ||y|| by H3 then H1 in B). Associativity of composition and bilinearity are those of linear maps between Banach spaces.
